name: Debug and Retry a Failed Workflow
query: Find my failed workflows from today and retry them
expected_behavior: Step 1: Check if `conductor` CLI is available
Step 2: Search for failed workflows using `conductor workflow search -s FAILED --start-time-after <today> -c 20`
Step 3: Present a summary table of failed workflows with workflowId, name, and failure reason
Step 4: For each failed workflow, get execution details to identify the failed task
Step 5: Show the failed task name, error message, and retry count
Step 6: Ask user which workflows to retry (or retry all if instructed)
Step 7: Retry each workflow using `conductor workflow retry <workflowId>`
Step 8: Verify each retried workflow status changed from FAILED to RUNNING
Step 9: Report results: how many retried successfully, any that failed to retry
success_criteria: Search command includes a time filter (e.g., --start-time-after with today's date or similar), not an unfiltered all-time search
Failed workflows are listed with relevant diagnostic info before retrying
Failed task details (task name, error, retry count) are extracted from execution
User is informed about failures before bulk retry (not blindly retried)
Retry command is used (not restart) to resume from the failed task
Post-retry status check confirms workflows are running again
Summary clearly shows success/failure count of retry operations
